hero.alt: A peregrine falcon perched majestically on a branch, showing its slate-grey back and barred breast
hero.caption: The peregrine falcon is the fastest animal in the world, capable of diving at over 200 mph.

habitat.fileTitle: File:Falco peregrinus minor, Cape Town, South Africa 118534019.jpg
habitat.alt: A peregrine falcon perched high on a rocky cliff face overlooking Cape Town
habitat.caption: They live in open habitats, nesting on high cliffs, mountains, and even skyscrapers in cities.

diet.alt: A peregrine falcon scanning its surroundings from a branch while holding a freshly caught bird
diet.caption: As carnivores, peregrine falcons hunt other birds, catching them mid-air in high-speed dives.

baby.fileTitle: File:Peregrine falcon (Falco peregrinus) and four chicks (ed4d091b-79a4-4752-be96-589ad25c39c7).jpg
baby.alt: A mother peregrine falcon feeding her four fluffy white chicks in a nest
baby.caption: Peregrine falcon chicks, called eyases, are born covered in fluffy white down.

family.alt: Two peregrine falcons perched near each other on a high ledge
family.caption: Falcons mate for life and return to the same nesting site, called an eyrie, every year.

range.alt: A peregrine falcon soaring with wings spread wide in a bright blue sky
range.caption: Peregrine falcons are found on every continent except Antarctica, making them one of the most widespread birds.

size.alt: A peregrine falcon perched, displaying its full body and tail length
size.caption: They are medium-sized birds, with a wingspan of about 3 to 4 feet (90 to 120 cm).

closeup.alt: Detailed close-up of a peregrine falcon's head showing its yellow beak and keen black eyes
closeup.caption: Their large eyes are specially protected by a third eyelid to help them see clearly during fast flights.

fun-fact.fileTitle: File:The Peregrine Falcon a Living Missile Bird.jpg
fun-fact.alt: A peregrine falcon in mid-flight diving like a missile
fun-fact.caption: When diving for prey, they fold their wings and tuck their legs, acting like an aerodynamic missile.

core-habitat.alt: A peregrine falcon perched on a rocky ledge
core-habitat.caption: High cliff ledges protect their nests from land predators like foxes.

core-diet.alt: A peregrine falcon on a post looking around after feeding
core-diet.caption: They require high-protein diets to maintain their high-energy flight style.

core-family.alt: A peregrine falcon calling out from its perch
core-family.caption: Falcons guard their nesting territories aggressively against other birds of prey.

core-baby.alt: A young peregrine falcon chick learning to stand in its rocky nest
core-baby.caption: Chicks grow rapidly and are ready to fly about 40 days after hatching.

core-range.fileTitle: File:Peregrine falcon (Falco peregrinus) in flight, Cape Baily, Kamay Botany Bay NSW Jun 2022.jpg
core-range.alt: A peregrine falcon gliding effortlessly in search of prey
core-range.caption: Their long-distance migrations are among the longest of any North American bird.
